shortDescription.text: Skill verified as legitimate with no malicious intent.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies skills that have been thoroughly analyzed and confirmed to be legitimate, serving their stated purpose without exhibiting any malicious behaviors, exploit primitives, or connections to known malicious infrastructure. Threat intelligence has been reconciled to support this benign classification.

message.text: The 'review' skill has been verified as legitimate. Threat intelligence strongly confirms its purpose as an AI-powered code review and remediation agent (e.g., https://github.com/awesome-skills/code-review-skill, https://claudearchitect.com/). No malicious intent, exploit primitives, or malicious infrastructure were identified. The observed behaviors (code analysis, git commands, file modification for fixes, task management) are consistent with its stated function. The skill explicitly aims to detect and fix security vulnerabilities like SQL injection, not introduce them. No confirmed malicious intent.
